info.name: Abroad Matrimony — Screen-Based API
info.description:
Complete API collection organised by app screen. Each folder = one Flutter screen. Import this collection alongside the environment file `screen-env.json`.

Base URL variable: `{{base_url}}` (default http://localhost:3000)
Auth token set automatically by the OTP Verify request via the post-response script.

variable.description: API base URL. Change to https://api.abroadmatrimony.com for production.
Auto-populated by OTP Verify or Token Refresh.
Auto-populated by OTP Verify or Token Refresh.
Test phone number in E.164 format.

item.name: 01 — Splash
item.description: App startup — verify the API is reachable before loading the main navigation.

request.description:
Called on app launch. Returns 200 + uptime when healthy. No auth required.

**Response fields:**
- `status` (string): Always "ok" when healthy
- `uptime` (number): Server uptime in seconds
- `timestamp` (ISO date-time): Server UTC time

item.name: 02 — Auth · Login Screen
item.description:
Phone OTP authentication flow. Call sequence:
1. Request OTP
2. Verify OTP → receive tokens
3. Use refresh token to renew access token

Alternative: trusted-device bypass (skip OTP) or magic link (email).

request.description:
Sends a 6-digit OTP to the phone via Twilio Verify.
Rate limit: **5 attempts per phone per 15 minutes**.

**Request fields:**
- `phone` (string, required): E.164 format e.g. +447911123456

**Response fields:**
- `data.otpSent` (boolean): true on success

Verifies the OTP. On success creates/retrieves the user row, issues JWT tokens, and registers the device.

**Request fields:**
- `phone` (string, required): Same E.164 phone from OTP request
- `code` (string, required): 6-digit OTP received via SMS
- `deviceFingerprint` (UUID, required): Stable UUID for this device install (stored in Flutter secure storage)

**Response fields:**
- `data.accessToken` (string): Short-lived JWT. Include as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. Expires in 15 min
- `data.refreshToken` (string): 30-day token. Use ONLY with the Token Refresh endpoint
- `data.expiresIn` (integer): TTL in seconds (always 900)
- `data.user.id` (UUID): User's unique ID
- `data.user.role` (string): USER | MODERATOR | ADMIN | SUPERADMIN
- `data.user.hasProfile` (boolean): false for new users → redirect to onboarding

Issues a new access + refresh token pair. The previous refresh token is immediately invalidated.
Call this when the API returns 401 on any request.

**Request fields:**
- `refreshToken` (string, required): The refresh token from the last auth response
- `deviceFingerprint` (UUID, required): Same device UUID used during OTP verify

**Errors:**
- 401: Token expired, already used, or reuse detected (all devices revoked)

Try this FIRST on app launch instead of showing the OTP screen. If the device was trusted within `TRUSTED_DEVICE_TTL_DAYS` (default 90 days), returns tokens immediately.

**Request fields:**
- `phone` (string, required): E.164 phone number
- `deviceFingerprint` (UUID, required): Device UUID from Flutter secure storage

**On 401:** Device not trusted or TTL expired → fall back to full OTP flow
**Rate limit:** 10 attempts per phone per hour

Call immediately after OTP verify. Returns a Firebase custom token.
Pass to `FirebaseAuth.signInWithCustomToken()` to enable real-time Firestore listeners (messages) and Realtime DB (presence).

**Response fields:**
- `data.firebaseToken` (string): Firebase custom token (1 hour TTL)

Revokes the current device's refresh token. Access token expires naturally after 15 min.

**Request fields:**
- `refreshToken` (string, required): The refresh token to revoke
Revokes ALL refresh tokens for this user across every registered device. Use when the user reports suspicious access.

Alternative login via email. Sends a one-time link that expires in 15 minutes.

**Request fields:**
- `email` (string, required): User's email address

Exchange the token from the magic link URL for JWT tokens.

**Request fields:**
- `token` (string, required): Token extracted from the magic link URL query parameter

item.name: 03 — Profile Setup · Onboarding
item.description:
Multi-step onboarding flow. Sequence:
1. Create Profile (basic info)
2. Answer 12 Real-Life Questions
3. Answer 3 Story Prompts
4. Upload Photo(s)
5. Upload Voice Intro (optional)

Call `GET /profile/me` at any time to check completion score.

body.raw:
{
  "fullName": "Priya Sharma",
  "dateOfBirth": "1993-08-15",
  "gender": "FEMALE",
  "currentCity": "London",
  "currentCountry": "United Kingdom",
  "settlementIntent": "STAY_HERE",
  "bio": "Software engineer who loves hiking and Bollywood classics.",
  "height": 165,
  "education": "MSc Computer Science, UCL",
  "occupation": "Senior Software Engineer",
  "motherTongue": "Hindi",
  "religion": "Hindu"
}

request.description:
Creates the user's profile. Call once after the user's first login.

**Request fields:**
- `fullName` (string, required, max 100): Display name
- `dateOfBirth` (date, required): YYYY-MM-DD — used to calculate displayed age
- `gender` (enum, required): MALE | FEMALE | NON_BINARY
- `currentCity` (string, required, max 100): City of residence
- `currentCountry` (string, required, max 100): Country — auto-joins REGIONAL group
- `settlementIntent` (enum, required): STAY_HERE | OPEN_TO_MOVE | RETURN_HOME | UNDECIDED
- `bio` (string, optional, max 500): Short bio on profile card
- `height` (integer, optional): Height in centimetres
- `education` (string, optional, max 200): Degree or field of study
- `occupation` (string, optional, max 200): Job title / employer
- `motherTongue` (string, optional): Primary language
- `religion` (string, optional): Religious background
- `caste` (string, optional): Community field — stored but not used in algorithm

Save or update one real-life question answer. Replace the path param with the question key.

**Path param `questionKey`** — one of:
- `FAITH_IMPORTANCE`: How important is faith/religion in daily life?
- `PARENTING_STYLE`: Philosophy on raising children
- `DIET_PREFERENCE`: Vegetarian / non-vegetarian / vegan etc.
- `EXERCISE_HABIT`: Fitness and activity level
- `FAMILY_STRUCTURE`: Joint family vs nuclear
- `PARENTS_INVOLVEMENT`: How much do parents factor into life decisions?
- `FINANCIAL_STYLE`: Saver vs spender, financial goals
- `CONFLICT_RESOLUTION`: Approach to disagreements
- `LIVING_PREFERENCE`: Urban / suburban / rural
- `SOCIAL_LIFE`: Introvert vs extrovert, social calendar
- `CAREER_AMBITION`: Career priority vs personal life
- `PET_PREFERENCE`: Attitude towards having pets

**Request fields:**
- `answer` (string, required, max 500): Free-text answer

Save or update one story prompt answer. Replace the path param with the prompt key.

**Path param `promptKey`** — one of:
- `HIGHLIGHT_MOMENT`: Tell us about a moment that shaped who you are
- `IDEAL_WEEKEND`: Describe your perfect weekend
- `LOOKING_FOR`: What are you genuinely looking for in a partner?

**Request fields:**
- `answer` (string, required, max 1000): Free-text story. Min ~50 chars recommended for quality AI analysis

formdata.description: Image file. JPEG, PNG, or WebP. Max 5 MB.

request.description:
Upload a profile photo. Uses `multipart/form-data`.

**Form fields:**
- `photo` (file, required): Image file. Accepted: JPEG, PNG, WebP. Max size: 5 MB

**Limits:** Max 6 photos per user

**Response fields:**
- `data.photoUrl` (string): CloudFront CDN URL of the uploaded photo
- `data.photoCount` (integer): Total photos on profile after upload

Step 1 of voice intro upload. Get a presigned S3 URL, then PUT the audio file directly to S3.

**Response fields:**
- `data.uploadUrl` (string): S3 presigned PUT URL (valid 15 min)
- `data.s3Key` (string): S3 key to pass to the Save Voice Intro endpoint after upload

Step 2 of voice intro upload. Call after successfully uploading the audio to S3.
Triggers Whisper transcription and AI profile intelligence update asynchronously.

**Request fields:**
- `s3Key` (string, required): The S3 key returned by the Get Upload URL endpoint

Returns the authenticated user's full profile including completion score (0–100), all answered questions, story prompts, and photo URLs.

**Response fields:**
- `data.completionScore` (integer): 0–100 — used in COMPLETE_PROFILE action queue item
- `data.realLifeAnswers` (object): Map of questionKey → answer
- `data.storyPrompts` (object): Map of promptKey → answer
- `data.photos` (array): CloudFront photo URLs
- `data.trustScore` (integer): Cached trust score
- `data.isPaused` (boolean): Whether profile is paused from discovery
- `data.isVerified` (boolean): Admin-approved ID verification status

item.name: 04 — Home · Discovery Feed
item.description: Main screen. Cursor-paginated list of ranked potential matches. Use `nextCursor` from the response as `cursor` in the next request.

query.description: Omit for first page. Use nextCursor from previous response.

request.description:
Returns a ranked, cursor-paginated list of potential matches personalised with the user's tuning weights.

**Query params:**
- `cursor` (string, optional): Pagination cursor from previous response
- `limit` (integer, optional, 1–100, default 20): Results per page

**Response fields:**
- `data.items[]` (array): Discovery profiles
  - `.userId` (UUID): Profile owner's UUID
  - `.fullName` (string): Display name
  - `.age` (integer): Calculated age
  - `.currentCity` (string): City of residence
  - `.currentCountry` (string): Country
  - `.thumbnailUrl` (string|null): First photo URL
  - `.totalScore` (float 0–1): Raw compatibility score
  - `.personalizedScore` (float 0–1): Score after tuning weights — feed sorted by this
  - `.scoreBreakdown` (object): 18-dimension breakdown
  - `.completionScore` (integer): Profile completeness of the match
  - `.isVerified` (boolean): ID verification status
- `data.nextCursor` (string|null): Pass as `cursor` in next request. Null = last page
- `data.total` (integer): Total eligible profiles in the pool

item.name: 05 — Profile Detail · Match View
item.description: Screens shown when the user taps a profile card from discovery or introductions.

request.description:
Returns the public view of another user's profile.
Privacy controls are enforced — bio, photos, or answers may be redacted if not mutually connected.

**Path param:** `id` — UUID of the user to view

**Response fields:**
- `data.photos` (array): Empty if `showPhotosBeforeMutual = false` and not connected
- `data.bio` (string|null): Null if `showBioBeforeMutual = false` and not connected
- `data.realLifeAnswers` (object|null): Null if `showAnswersBeforeMutual = false`
- `data.isVerified` (boolean): Admin-approved verification status
- `data.trustScore` (integer): Public trust score

Records that the authenticated user viewed this profile. Call when the profile detail screen opens.
Deduplicated with a 1-hour window — same viewer-profile pair only logs once per hour.
Contributes to `profileViewMomentum` matching dimension and Signals dashboard.

**Errors:**
- 400: Cannot view your own profile (ViewSelfError)

Returns compatibility breakdown (18 dimensions) + why-this-match highlight cards.

**Response fields:**
- `data.totalScore` (float): Raw compatibility score (0–1)
- `data.personalizedScore` (float): Score after tuning
- `data.breakdown` (object): 18-dimension `ScoreBreakdown` object
- `data.whyThisMatch.cards[]` (array, max 3):
  - `.dimension` (string): Dimension key
  - `.label` (string): Human-readable name e.g. "Settlement Plans"
  - `.score` (float): Score for this dimension
  - `.highlight` (string): One-sentence explanation
- `data.whyThisMatch.narrative` (string|null): LLM paragraph (Redis-cached 24h)

item.name: 06 — Introductions · Drop Screen
item.description: Weekly curated introduction drops. Multiple themed drops can be live simultaneously.

request.description:
Returns all LIVE drops visible to the user with early-access and unlock state.

**Response fields per drop:**
- `id` (UUID): Drop identifier
- `title` (string): Theme name
- `releaseAt` (ISO date-time): When profiles become public
- `earlyAccessCost` (integer): Diamonds to VIEW before release
- `unlockCost` (integer): Diamonds to UNLOCK full profile
- `introductionCount` (integer): Your assigned introductions in this drop
- `hasEarlyAccess` (boolean): Already paid for early access?
- `isUnlocked` (boolean): Already unlocked?
- `status`: DRAFT | PENDING_APPROVAL | SCHEDULED | LIVE | EXPIRED

Returns drop metadata + assigned introductions (profiles may be blurred until early-access is purchased).

**Path param:** `dropId` — Drop UUID

Spend `earlyAccessCost` diamonds to VIEW profiles in this drop before the release date.
Idempotent — safe to call multiple times.

**Errors:**
- 402: Insufficient diamonds
- 409: Already have early access

Spend `unlockCost` diamonds to fully reveal an introduction's profile in this drop.
Requires early access first.

**Errors:**
- 402: Insufficient diamonds
Returns full detail of one introduction: profile summary, score breakdown, and why-this-match card.

item.name: Unlock All Current-Week Intros Early (300 diamonds)

request.description:
Spend 300 diamonds (`EARLY_UNLOCK_DIAMOND_COST`) to unlock all current-week introductions.
Idempotent — does not charge twice.

**Errors:**
- 402: Insufficient diamonds (EarlyUnlockInsufficientDiamondsError)
Returns introductions from the current week's drops (legacy endpoint). Use the Drops endpoints above for full drop management.

item.name: 07 — Groups · Community
item.description: Four group types: REGIONAL (auto-join), CULTURAL, PROFESSIONAL, INTEREST. Social feed with posts, comments, and likes.

request.description: Returns all groups the authenticated user has joined.
Groups the user hasn't joined, ranked by country match + member count. Max controlled by SystemConfig.SUGGESTED_GROUPS_MAX (default 20).
Curated group list for the end-of-onboarding 'Join some groups' screen.
Returns group detail including type, member count, description, and recent activity.

Join a group. REGIONAL groups are auto-joined on registration — this is for manual joins of CULTURAL/PROFESSIONAL/INTEREST groups.

**Errors:**
- 409: Already a member (AlreadyInGroupError)
Leave a group.
Paginated list of group members. All member lists are visible in all group types.
Returns posts sorted by isPinned DESC, createdAt DESC. Includes like count and comment count per post.

Create a text post in the group feed.

**Request fields:**
- `content` (string, required, max 2000): Post text. Plain text only, no HTML
Like a post. Idempotent — calling twice does not create duplicate likes.
Unlike a post. Idempotent.

Add a flat comment to a post. No nested threads.

**Request fields:**
- `content` (string, required, max 500): Comment text
Returns all comments on a post. Flat list ordered by createdAt ASC.

Propose a new INTEREST group. Goes to admin review queue.

**Request fields:**
- `name` (string, required, max 100): Proposed group name
- `description` (string, required, max 500): Purpose and audience
- `tags` (array of strings, optional): Topic tags

On admin approval: Group is created and proposer is auto-joined.

item.name: 08 — Events · Gatherings
item.description: Community events — listing, calendar view, RSVP. Events feed and calendar shown on home screen.

query.description: Filter by event tag e.g. networking, cultural

request.description:
Returns events with a personalised `whyInvited` reason for each.

**Query params:**
- `upcoming` (boolean, optional): Filter to future events
- `limit` (integer, optional, default 20)
- `tag` (string, optional): Filter by event category tag

**Response fields per event:**
- `whyInvited` (string): Personalised reason (e.g., 'You're in the London group hosting this event')

Returns this week's milestone timeline: intro drops, check-ins, prompt deadlines, and events.

**Response fields per milestone:**
- `type` (enum): INTRO_DROP | CHECK_IN | PROMPT_DEADLINE | EVENT
- `title` (string): Display title
- `scheduledAt` (ISO date-time): When it happens
- `referenceId` (UUID|null): Related entity ID
Returns full event detail including description, venue, organisers, and RSVP counts.
Mark as GOING. Co-attendance with another GOING user contributes to the `eventCoAttendance` matching dimension (processed after the event by the admin).
Remove GOING status.

item.name: 09 — Weekly Prompts
item.description: Community prompt — one active per week set by admin. Users respond and can resonate with others' responses.

request.description:
Returns the currently active weekly prompt.

**Response fields:**
- `data.id` (UUID): Prompt ID
- `data.question` (string): The prompt question
- `data.activeFrom` (ISO date-time): When this prompt became active
- `data.activeTo` (ISO date-time): When this prompt expires
- `data.hasResponded` (boolean): Whether the authenticated user has already responded

**Errors:**
- 404: No active prompt this week

Submit a response to the current prompt. Published to the community feed.

**Request fields:**
- `type` (enum, required): TEXT | AUDIO
- `content` (string, required when type=TEXT, max 1000): Written response text
- `audioS3Key` (string, required when type=AUDIO): S3 key of uploaded audio file

Contributes to `promptResonance` matching dimension when another user resonates with your response.
Returns paginated community responses to the current prompt.

Mark that this response resonated with you.

**Matching impact:**
- Mutual resonate (both users resonated with each other's responses): `promptResonance = 1.0`
- One-way resonate: `promptResonance = 0.5`
- No resonate: `promptResonance = 0.0`
Un-resonate with a response.

item.name: 10 — Conversations · Chat
item.description: REST fallback for Firestore real-time chat. Flutter listens to Firestore directly for live updates; these endpoints handle history, send, read receipts, media URLs, and flagging.

request.description:
Returns all conversation threads with last-message preview and unread count.

**Response fields per conversation:**
- `id` (UUID): Conversation ID
- `otherUser` (object): Profile summary of the other participant
- `lastMessage` (object): Preview of the most recent message
- `unreadCount` (integer): Messages not yet read

query.description: ISO date-time — fetch messages before this timestamp (reverse pagination)

request.description:
Paginated message history. Primary source is Firestore; use this as fallback.

**Query params:**
- `limit` (integer, default 50): Messages per page
- `before` (ISO date-time, optional): Fetch messages before this timestamp

**Response fields per message:**
- `id` (UUID): Message ID
- `type` (enum): TEXT | IMAGE | AUDIO
- `content` (string): Text content (empty for media)
- `mediaUrl` (string|null): CloudFront URL for IMAGE/AUDIO
- `durationSeconds` (integer|null): Audio duration for AUDIO messages
- `senderId` (UUID): Sender's user ID
- `createdAt` (ISO date-time)

Send a message. For IMAGE or AUDIO, upload to S3 first via the Upload URL endpoint.

**Request fields:**
- `type` (enum, required): TEXT | IMAGE | AUDIO
- `content` (string, required for TEXT, max 2000): Message text
- `mediaUrl` (string, required for IMAGE/AUDIO): CloudFront URL of uploaded file
- `durationSeconds` (integer, required for AUDIO): Audio length in seconds

Triggers FCM push notification if recipient is offline (respects quiet window 22:00–07:00 local time).

query.description: MIME type of the file to upload e.g. image/jpeg, audio/mp4

request.description:
Get a presigned S3 PUT URL for uploading a media file before sending.

**Query params:**
- `mimeType` (string, required): MIME type of the upload

**Response fields:**
- `data.uploadUrl` (string): S3 presigned PUT URL (valid 15 min)
- `data.publicUrl` (string): CloudFront URL to use in the Send Message request
REST fallback for updating read receipt. Firestore handles this in real-time normally.

Report a message for moderation.

**Request fields:**
- `reason` (enum, required): HARASSMENT | SPAM | HATE_SPEECH | INAPPROPRIATE_CONTENT | SCAM | OTHER
- `details` (string, optional, max 500): Additional context

After 3 flags from different users, message is auto-hidden in Firestore.

item.name: 11 — Connections
item.description: Send, manage, and list connection requests. Accepting creates a Match record and opens a conversation.

request.description:
Send a connection request.

**Request fields:**
- `targetUserId` (UUID, required): The user to connect with
- `message` (string, optional, max 300): Personal note included with the request

List connections filtered by status.

**Query params:**
- `status` (enum, optional): PENDING | ACCEPTED | DECLINED
Accept an incoming connection request. Creates a mutual Match record and opens a conversation thread.
Decline an incoming connection request. The sender is not notified.
Withdraw a connection request you sent (only while still PENDING).

item.name: 12 — Habits · Consistency Hub
item.description: 10 preset habits. Log completions, view streaks, weekly dots, history charts, and AI reflection.

request.description:
Returns the catalogue of all 10 habits with the user's current streak.

**Habit keys:** MORNING_ROUTINE, EXERCISE, HEALTHY_EATING, MEDITATION, READING, JOURNALING, LEARNING, FAMILY_TIME, SOCIAL_CONNECTION, GRATITUDE

item.name: All Habits with Streaks + Week Dots

request.description:
Returns all habits enriched with streak data and weekly dot pattern.

**Response fields per habit:**
- `habitKey` (string): Habit identifier
- `currentStreak` (integer): Consecutive days logged
- `longestStreak` (integer): All-time best streak
- `thisWeekDots` (boolean[7]): Mon=index0 → Sun=index6. True = logged that day
- `loggedToday` (boolean): Whether the user has already logged today

Log completion of a habit for today. Replace `EXERCISE` in the path with any habit key.

**Path param:** `habitKey` — MORNING_ROUTINE | EXERCISE | HEALTHY_EATING | MEDITATION | READING | JOURNALING | LEARNING | FAMILY_TIME | SOCIAL_CONNECTION | GRATITUDE

**Request fields:**
- `date` (date, optional): YYYY-MM-DD — defaults to today UTC
- `note` (string, optional, max 200): Brief note about this session

Remove a habit log for a specific date. Recalculates streak automatically.

**Path params:**
- `habitKey`: Habit identifier
- `date`: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD)
Get streak data for a single habit.

query.description: Number of past weeks. Default 8, max 52.

request.description:
Returns per-week completion data for a bar/dot chart.

**Query params:**
- `weeks` (integer, default 8, max 52): Historical weeks to return

Returns a rule-based or AI-generated insight about the user's habit patterns this week.
Redis-cached for 7 days per user.

**Response fields:**
- `data.insight` (string): The reflection text
- `data.topHabit` (string|null): The most consistent habit key
- `data.streakHighlight` (string|null): Notable streak achievement

item.name: Toggle Habit Summary Visibility on Profile

request.description:
Show or hide the habit consistency summary on the public profile card.

**Request fields:**
- `visible` (boolean, required): true = show on profile, false = hide

Save a personal reflection note.

**Request fields:**
- `habitKey` (string, optional): Link reflection to a specific habit
- `note` (string, required, max 1000): Reflection text

item.name: 13 — Signals · Dashboard
item.description: Personal activity metrics shown on the dashboard screen.

request.description:
Returns 4 weekly metrics compared to the previous week.

**Response fields per metric (profileViews, connectionRequests, promptResonates, introPoolSize):**
- `thisWeek` (integer): Count for current week (Mon–Sun UTC)
- `lastWeek` (integer): Count for previous week
- `delta` (integer): thisWeek - lastWeek (positive = growth)
- `deltaPercent` (float|null): Percentage change. Null if lastWeek is 0

Returns prioritised items requiring the user's attention, sorted by urgency:
1. RESPOND_TO_INTRO — unanswered introduction in current drop
2. ACCEPT_CONNECTION — pending incoming connection request
3. COMPLETE_PROFILE — completion score below 80%

**Response fields per item:**
- `type` (enum): RESPOND_TO_INTRO | ACCEPT_CONNECTION | COMPLETE_PROFILE
- `title` (string): Display title
- `description` (string): Action description
- `referenceId` (UUID|null): Related entity to navigate to
- `priority` (integer): Sort order (lower = higher priority)

Returns daily profile view counts for the past 7 days, oldest first.
Use for a sparkline chart on the dashboard.

**Response fields:**
- `data.days` (integer[7]): View counts per day. Index 0 = 7 days ago, index 6 = today.

item.name: 14 — Saved Profiles · Shortlist
item.description: Bookmark profiles with labels and notes. Compare up to 3 side by side.

request.description:
Add a profile to the shortlist.

**Request fields:**
- `savedUserId` (UUID, required): Profile to save
- `label` (enum, optional): FAVOURITE | MAYBE | NOT_NOW — default MAYBE
- `notes` (string, optional, max 500): Private note only you can see

query.description: FAVOURITE | MAYBE | NOT_NOW — omit for all

request.description: Returns saved profiles filtered by label.

query.description: Comma-separated list of 2–3 user UUIDs. All must be in your saved list.

request.description:
Side-by-side comparison of real-life answers for 2–3 saved profiles.

**Query params:**
- `ids` (string, required): Comma-separated UUIDs, 2–3 profiles

**Errors:**
- 404: One or more UUIDs not in your saved list (ProfileNotSavedError)

Update the label or notes on a saved profile.

**Request fields (all optional — send only what you want to change):**
- `label` (enum): FAVOURITE | MAYBE | NOT_NOW
- `notes` (string, max 500)

Add or replace the private note on a saved profile.

**Request fields:**
- `note` (string, required, max 500): New note text (replaces existing)
Remove a profile from the saved shortlist.

item.name: 15 — Trust Centre · Privacy
item.description: Trust score dashboard, privacy settings, pause/resume, block, and report.

request.description:
Returns the composite trust score and all 6 layers.
Score is persisted to Profile.trustScore on every call.

**Response fields:**
- `data.score` (integer 0–100): Composite trust score
- `data.layers[]`: Array of trust layers:
  - `key`: PHONE | PROFILE_COMPLETE | PHOTO | ID_VERIFIED | EMAIL | VOICE
  - `points` (integer): Points earned (0 if not earned)
  - `maxPoints` (integer): Max available from this layer
  - `earned` (boolean): Whether the user has completed this layer
  - `description` (string): What to do to earn these points
- `data.isPaused` (boolean): Profile pause status
- `data.privacySettings.showPhotosBeforeMutual` (boolean)
- `data.privacySettings.showBioBeforeMutual` (boolean)
- `data.privacySettings.showAnswersBeforeMutual` (boolean)

Partial-merge update — only include fields you want to change.

**Request fields (all optional):**
- `showPhotosBeforeMutual` (boolean): Show photos before mutual connection? Default true
- `showBioBeforeMutual` (boolean): Show bio/prompts before mutual connection? Default true
- `showAnswersBeforeMutual` (boolean): Show real-life answers before connection? Default true

Sets Profile.isPaused = true. Profile disappears from discovery feeds and introduction pools.
Existing connections and conversations are NOT affected.
Sets Profile.isPaused = false. Profile reappears in discovery feeds.
Returns static definitions of PUBLIC / TRUSTED / FAMILY access levels and their visible fields. No DB call — pure function.

Block a user. Blocked users cannot view your profile, send messages, or appear in discovery.

**Request fields:**
- `targetUserId` (UUID, required): User to block
Unblock a previously blocked user.
Returns all users blocked by the authenticated user.

Report a user to the moderation team.

**Request fields:**
- `targetUserId` (UUID, required): User to report
- `reason` (enum, required): FAKE_PROFILE | HARASSMENT | SPAM | INAPPROPRIATE_CONTENT | SCAM | OTHER
- `details` (string, optional, max 1000): Additional context for moderators

item.name: 16 — Payments · Membership & Diamonds
item.description: Founding Member subscription (Stripe global / Razorpay India) and diamond currency.

request.description:
Returns the user's Founding Member status.

**Response fields:**
- `data.isActive` (boolean): Whether an active subscription exists
- `data.plan` (string|null): Plan name
- `data.currentPeriodEnd` (ISO date-time|null): Renewal date
- `data.cancelAtPeriodEnd` (boolean): Whether subscription will cancel at renewal

Creates a Stripe Checkout session for the Founding Member subscription.
Redirect user to `checkoutUrl` in a WebView.

**Response fields:**
- `data.checkoutUrl` (string): Stripe-hosted checkout page URL
- `data.sessionId` (string): Stripe session ID for tracking

Creates a Razorpay order for users in India. Pass `orderId` to the Razorpay Flutter SDK.

**Response fields:**
- `data.orderId` (string): Razorpay order ID → pass to Flutter SDK
- `data.amount` (integer): Amount in paise (₹ × 100)
- `data.currency` (string): Always INR

Call after the Razorpay Flutter SDK returns a successful payment callback.

**Request fields:**
- `razorpayPaymentId` (string, required): From SDK success callback
- `razorpayOrderId` (string, required): From the create-order step
- `razorpaySignature` (string, required): HMAC-SHA256 signature from SDK — verified server-side

Returns current diamond balance and recent transactions.

**Response fields:**
- `data.balance` (integer): Current diamonds
- `data.recentTransactions[]`:
  - `delta` (integer): +credit / -debit
  - `balanceAfter` (integer): Balance after this transaction
  - `reason` (string): Ledger reason code
  - `createdAt` (ISO date-time)

Create a Stripe Checkout session for a diamond package.

**Request fields:**
- `packageId` (enum, required):
  - `STARTER_50`: 50 diamonds
  - `POPULAR_150`: 150 diamonds (most popular)
  - `VALUE_350`: 350 diamonds
  - `PREMIUM_750`: 750 diamonds

Deduct diamonds from balance. Appends a debit row to the append-only ledger.

**Request fields:**
- `amount` (integer, required, min 1): Diamonds to spend
- `reason` (enum, required): INTRO_EARLY_VIEW | INTRO_EARLY_UNLOCK | GROUP_CONVERSATION_INITIATION | CUSTOM
- `referenceId` (UUID, optional): Related entity (drop ID, group ID etc.)

**Errors:**
- 402: Insufficient diamonds (InsufficientDiamondsError)

item.name: 17 — Verification · Identity
item.description: ID document + selfie verification. Submit → admin review → approved/rejected.

item.name: Get Upload URL (ID doc or selfie)

request.description:
Get a presigned S3 PUT URL for uploading the verification document or selfie.
Call twice — once for `ID_DOCUMENT`, once for `SELFIE`.

**Query params:**
- `type` (enum, required): ID_DOCUMENT | SELFIE

**Response fields:**
- `data.uploadUrl` (string): S3 presigned PUT URL (valid 15 min)
- `data.s3Key` (string): S3 key to pass to Submit Verification

Submit for admin review. Creates a VerificationRequest in PENDING status.

**Request fields:**
- `idDocumentS3Key` (string, required): S3 key from ID_DOCUMENT upload
- `selfieS3Key` (string, required): S3 key from SELFIE upload
- `documentType` (enum, required): PASSPORT | DRIVING_LICENCE | NATIONAL_ID

On admin approval: +25 trust score points, `isVerified = true` on profile.

Poll for verification status.

**Response fields:**
- `data.status` (enum): NOT_SUBMITTED | PENDING | APPROVED | REJECTED
- `data.submittedAt` (ISO date-time|null)
- `data.reviewedAt` (ISO date-time|null)
- `data.rejectionReason` (string|null): Admin's reason if REJECTED
- `data.trustScore` (integer): Current trust score

item.name: 18 — Match Tuning · Preferences
item.description: Two-question preference UI that adjusts algorithm weights. Preview rank impact before saving.

request.description:
Returns current tuning preferences as two 1–5 ratings.

**Response fields:**
- `data.settlementImportance` (integer 1–5): Current setting. Default 3
- `data.familyImportance` (integer 1–5): Current setting. Default 3
- `data.settlementMultiplier` (float): Applied multiplier (1=×0.5, 3=×1.0, 5=×2.5)
- `data.familyMultiplier` (float): Applied multiplier
- `data.lastUpdatedAt` (ISO date-time|null): When last saved

Preview the rank change WITHOUT saving. Applies proposed weights to top 20 stored MatchScore pairs.

**Query params:**
- `settlementImportance` (integer 1–5, required)
- `familyImportance` (integer 1–5, required)

**Response fields:**
- `data.profilesUp` (integer): Profiles that would rank higher
- `data.profilesDown` (integer): Profiles that would rank lower
- `data.profilesUnchanged` (integer)
- `data.topGainers[]`: Profiles gaining the most rank

Save tuning preferences. Enqueues a background score recompute for all stored match pairs.

**Request fields:**
- `settlementImportance` (integer 1–5, required): How important same-country settlement plans are
- `familyImportance` (integer 1–5, required): How important family involvement is

**Multiplier scale:** 1=×0.50, 2=×0.75, 3=×1.00, 4=×1.75, 5=×2.50

item.name: 19 — Admin Panel
item.description: Admin-only endpoints. Requires admin JWT from POST /admin/auth/login. Role required varies per endpoint.

request.description:
Admin login requires email + password (bcrypt) + 6-digit TOTP from authenticator app.

**Request fields:**
- `email` (string, required): Admin account email
- `password` (string, required): Admin password
- `totp` (string, required): 6-digit code from TOTP authenticator app
Paginated user list. Requires MODERATOR+ role.
Full user detail including profile, verification status, device list, payment history.

Suspend a user. Logs to audit trail.

**Request fields:**
- `reason` (string, required): Reason logged in audit trail
Returns DAU, MAU, new registrations, active memberships, diamond revenue, and retention rates. Requires ADMIN+ role.
Returns all feature flags with rollout percentage and allowlists.

Create a feature flag.

**Request fields:**
- `key` (string, required): Flag identifier
- `enabled` (boolean, required): Global on/off
- `rolloutPercent` (integer 0–100, optional): Percentage-based rollout
- `allowedUserIds` (UUID[], optional): Specific users always getting this flag
- `allowedEnvironments` (string[], optional): Environments where active
List verification requests filtered by status. Requires MODERATOR+ role.
Approve an ID verification request. Adds 25 trust score points and sets isVerified = true on the profile.
All key-value system config entries. Examples: SUGGESTED_GROUPS_MAX, INTRO_DIAMOND_COST.

Create or update a system config value.

**Request fields:**
- `key` (string, required): Config key
- `value` (string, required): String value
- `description` (string, optional): Human-readable description
- `isProtected` (boolean, optional): If true, key cannot be deleted

Trigger AI to propose DRAFT introduction drops for a region.
Requires OPENAI_API_KEY. Creates IntroductionDrop rows in DRAFT status for admin review.

**Request fields:**
- `region` (string, required): Country/region to generate drops for
